key_insight: Everyday human-AI interaction can act as a neuroplastic training loop, where the usual re-prompt cycle may reinforce reactive habits unless a brief observational pause interrupts it.
engineering_relevance: The paper frames human-AI systems as behavior-shaping environments, suggesting interface and workflow designs that can reduce frustration-driven spirals and support more adaptive user responses.
method: Model the interaction loop as request → result → appraisal → revision.
Insert a brief “regulatory gap” where the user observes the reactive response instead of immediately re-prompting.
Offer two application modes: user-guided observation without tool changes, and agent-assisted observation with light configuration.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents the framework qualitatively and illustrates it with generative image prompting, comparing observed versus unobserved sessions rather than reporting quantitative evaluation.
watch_item: The approach depends on users noticing and sustaining the pre-cognitive pause, which may be difficult to implement consistently without careful interface design.
paper_title: Human-AI Agent Interaction as a Neuroplastic Training Environment
